# ferriskey_user_role (Resource)

Assigns a realm role to a user (or a client's service account user). The assignment is a pure link: any change forces it to be recreated. Commonly used to grant a Terraform service account its scoped admin roles during bootstrap.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Grant a client's service account a built-in admin role (bootstrap pattern).
data "ferriskey_role" "staff" {
  realm = "master"
  name  = "staff"
}

resource "ferriskey_client" "runner" {
  realm                   = "master"
  client_id               = "terraform-runner"
  client_type             = "confidential"
  service_account_enabled = true
}

resource "ferriskey_user_role" "runner_staff" {
  realm   = "master"
  user_id = ferriskey_client.runner.service_account_user_id
  role_id = data.ferriskey_role.staff.id
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `realm` (String) Realm the user and role belong to. Changing this forces a new resource.
- `role_id` (String) UUID of the role to assign. Changing this forces a new resource.
- `user_id` (String) UUID of the user (e.g. a client's `service_account_user_id`). Changing this forces a new resource.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Composite identifier `{realm}/{user_id}/{role_id}`.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Imported with the composite ID "{realm}/{user_id}/{role_id}".
terraform import ferriskey_user_role.runner_staff master/USER_UUID/ROLE_UUID
```
